slides.eyebrow: Education · International markets — what UK, US, and EU actually want
slides.headline: Your music can work in London. New York. Paris.
Here's what you need to know:
slides.lede: But what "works" means is different in each market. ↓ · — — — · THE UK MARKET
list_items: But what "works" means is different in each market. ↓

slides.headline: Here's what you need to know:
slides.lede:
UK music supervisors are ahead on African music. They've placed it.
What they're specifically requesting: · Afrobeats and Afropop for lifestyle, fashion, food content · Highlife and Afrojuju for documentary and cultural programming · Contemporary orchestral work from African composers for drama
list_items:
UK music supervisors are ahead on African music. They've placed it.
What they're specifically requesting:
Afrobeats and Afropop for lifestyle, fashion, food content
Highlife and Afrojuju for documentary and cultural programming
Contemporary orchestral work from African composers for drama
Roots reggae and Afro-soul for charitable campaigns

slides.headline: Turnaround expectation: 24–48 hours from brief.
slides.lede:
Rights requirement: one-stop cleared (master + sync in one conversation).
Stems: always requested — deliver without being asked. · Here's what you need to know: — — — · THE US MARKET

slides.headline: Here's what you need to know:
slides.lede:
The US sync market is larger but harder to enter without an agent or verified track record.
What breaks through: · High-energy Afrobeats for sports content (NBA, NFL campaigns, streaming sports docs) · Minimal, emotive African acoustic for healthcare, insurance, social campaigns · African electronic for tech and automotive
list_items:
The US sync market is larger but harder to enter without an agent or verified track record.
What breaks through:
High-energy Afrobeats for sports content (NBA, NFL campaigns, streaming sports docs)
Minimal, emotive African acoustic for healthcare, insurance, social campaigns
African electronic for tech and automotive

slides.headline: US briefs come with tighter formatting requirements.
slides.lede: Metadata completeness is non-negotiable — tracks with missing ISRC or split sheet fail submission. · Here's what you need to know: — — — · THE EU MARKET

slides.headline: Here's what you need to know:
slides.lede:
European campaigns have been the earliest adopters of African music.
German and French markets specifically request: · African choral music for brand storytelling · Contemporary Afropop for consumer goods · African jazz for finance and luxury
list_items:
European campaigns have been the earliest adopters of African music.
German and French markets specifically request:
African choral music for brand storytelling
African jazz for finance and luxury

slides.headline:
EU campaigns often run multi-territory.
Your sync fee should reflect it.
slides.lede: Here's what you need to know: — — — · WHAT ALL THREE MARKETS REQUIRE
